demographics.audit_notes: Values for total_population and median_age adjusted slightly to match 1990 Decennial Census data for Tract 24.41 precisely. Original values were within 1%.
All values are plausible linear interpolations between the 1990 and 2000 Decennial Census data points.
Values adjusted slightly to match 2000 Decennial Census data for Tract 24.41 precisely. Original values were within 1%.
All values are plausible linear interpolations between 2000 Census and 2006-2010 ACS data.
Values aligned with 2010 Decennial Census and ACS 2006-2010 data. Original values were within 1-2% and considered high confidence but updated for precision.

Corrected percent_white (was 66.8) and percent_hispanic (was 24.5) to align with ACS 2011-2015 data. The original values incorrectly showed a sharp reversal of the Hispanic population's growth trend for this period.
Values aligned with 2020 Decennial Census and the most recent ACS 5-Year estimates (2018-2022) centered near 2020. Original values were very close.
Values are plausible extrapolations from the 2020 baseline using recent trends.
Values are plausible extrapolations from the 2020 baseline. Median age is centered on the latest ACS release.
Values are projections based on recent trends. Confidence is medium as they represent a future estimate.
Values are projections based on recent trends. Confidence is medium as they represent a future estimate.
Values are projections based on recent trends. Confidence is medium as they represent a future estimate.

property.audit_notes: Baseline year. All values align with 1990 Census data for the tract.
Values are plausible interpolations between 1990 and 2000 census data points.
Corrected median_home_value (was 140000) and average_rent (was 800) to precisely match 2000 Census data.
Values are plausible interpolations between 2000 Census and 2010 ACS data.
Corrected median_home_value (was 240000) and average_rent (was 1100) to align with ACS 2006-2010 data.
Corrected values to align with ACS 2011-2015 data. Original values were plausible but slightly high.
Corrected values to align with ACS 2018-2022 data, which is centered on 2020. Original values were very close.
The massive 27.4% YoY increase in median home value is accurate and reflects the post-COVID housing market boom in Austin. Flagged for the significant spike.

Corrected median_home_value from $850,000 to $790,000. The original value was likely based on peak asking prices, while TCAD and Zillow data suggest a slightly lower median for the entire year before the market cooled.
Projected values reflect a market stabilization/slight rebound after the 2022 peak, which is plausible.
Projected values showing modest growth, consistent with market forecasts.
Projected values showing modest growth, consistent with market forecasts.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: Corrected income and education to precisely match 1990 Census. Newly added fields are estimated based on available data for the period.
Values are plausible interpolations between 1990 and 2000 census data points.
Corrected values to precisely match 2000 Census data. Original values were very close.
Values are plausible interpolations between 2000 Census and 2010 ACS data.
Corrected values to align with ACS 2006-2010 data. Original values were very close.
Corrected values to align with ACS 2011-2015 data. Original values were very close.
Corrected values to align with ACS 2018-2022 data. Eviction filing rate reflects impact of COVID-19 moratoriums.
Values are plausible extrapolations from the 2020 baseline.

Corrected median_household_income from $138,000 to $129,000. The original value represented an implausibly large one-year jump. The corrected value is more in line with the upper bound of the latest ACS data.
Projected values adjusted to follow the corrected 2022 baseline. Original income projection of $142,000 was too high.
Projected values adjusted to follow the corrected 2022 baseline. Original income projection of $146,000 was too high.
Projected values adjusted to follow the corrected 2022 baseline. Original income projection of $150,000 was too high.
